page: Amazon Web Services m7i.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon 8488C cores with hyper-threading enabled. Operating at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz, the processor includes 105 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and AVX-512. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core capabilities, with the instance achieving top-tier results in single-threaded gzip compression and Geekbench single-core and text processing tests. However, multi-core and memory-intensive operations show some limitations. Multi-core performance in stress-ng and bzip3 decompression falls into the lower tier, as does uncached large-block memory read bandwidth. Passmark database operations and Redis SET operations also register in the lower performance quartile, reflecting a tradeoff where single-threaded execution speed outpaces parallelized database and memory-throughput workloads.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m7i.xlarge offers a modern DDR5 memory architecture and dedicated Intel Xeon compute resources without the premium of specialized memory or storage-optimized hardware. It is best suited for general-purpose workloads that benefit from strong single-thread performance, such as web serving, text processing, and light serialization tasks. It is less optimal for highly parallelized database operations, large-scale software compilation, or memory-bandwidth-bound applications.
description: Amazon Web Services m7i.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it includes 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core execution, particularly in text processing and single-threaded compression. However, multi-core scaling and uncached memory bandwidth are relatively weak, as evidenced by lower-tier results in multi-threaded decompression and database operations. Qualitatively, the instance provides a balanced, cost-efficient profile for workloads requiring modern instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512 without the overhead of specialized hardware. It is well-suited for static web serving, text processing, and light serialization, but less effective for highly parallelized database tasks or intensive software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong single-core performance for general workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated general-purpose virtual machine featuring high-speed DDR5 memory and strong single-core performance for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 4 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8488C processor at 3.2 GHz
Equipped with 16.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 advanced instruction sets
Delivers strong single-core performance in text processing and compression
Features a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps with no local storage
Best suited for static web serving, text processing, and light serialization
